items.title: 'We will be ready regardless!' | Rashford and Rogers defiant amid kick-off time chaos
items.summary: Morgan Rogers and Marcus Rashford spoke to the media after it was announced that England's kick-off time could be moved.

items.title: Liverpool open to Van Dijk offers - Saturday's gossip
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk's Liverpool future appears a little uncertain with the club prepared to listen to offers for him, Fulham keen on West Ham winger Crysencio Summerville while Inter Milan want Chelsea centre-back Trevoh Chalobah.

items.title: Australia 1 Egypt 1 (2-4 on pens): Pharaohs win first-ever World Cup knockout game to reach last-16
items.summary: MO Salah and Egypt buried the penalty curse of the Pharoahs to make their own history. Under Salah’s captaincy, the team had twice tasted spotkick agony on the big occasion against Senegal - once in an AFCON final and then in a World Cup play-off. items.title: Wilder would welcome Usyk fight, says co-manager
items.summary: Deontay Wilder's co-manager Shelly Finkel has confirmed that the American former world heavyweight champion would welcome a fight against Oleksandr Usyk.

items.title: Egypt eliminate Australia from World Cup after penalty shoot-out drama
items.summary: Egypt are into the last 16 of the World Cup after beating Australia in a dramatic penalty shoot-out in Dallas.

items.title: Rice likely to be available for England v Mexico
items.summary: Declan Rice is likely to be available to start England's World Cup tie against Mexico despite an ongoing injury issue.

items.title: 'I decided last minute!' - Salah on Panenka penalty
items.summary: Mo Salah explains it was a last-minute decision to "Panenka" his penalty for Egypt after they beat Australia in a shootout to progress to the last 16 of the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo in touching gesture to young fan who lost everything in devastating Venezuela earthquakes
items.summary: CRISTIANO RONALDO has sent a touching message to a Venezuelan boy who lost everything in a recent earthquake. items.summary: GARY Lineker weighed up the question for a moment. As one of our greatest-ever strikers, who scored 48 goals in 80 appearances for his country, where did he rank captain Harry Kane in English football folklore? items.title: Postecoglou announced as Al Nassr head coach
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has signed a two-year contract as coach of Saudi Pro League champions Al Nassr, linking up with Cristiano Ronaldo at the Riyadh-based team.

items.title: Ancelotti: SAF is only manager more qualified
items.summary: Brazil manager Carlo Ancelotti had a reply to social media doubters in Brazil on Friday, saying legendary former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson is the only manager more qualified than he is.

items.title: Sabalenka sets up mouth-watering Wimbledon clash with Osaka
items.summary: Aryna Sabalenka set up a mouth-watering Wimbledon clash with Naomi Osaka as Coco Gauff matched her best run, while Novak Djokovic and Jannik Sinner continue their title pursuit.

items.title: England, Mexico & Fifa locked in kick-off time talks
items.summary: England's World Cup last-16 tie with Mexico is set to be moved to 19:00 BST on Sunday.

items.title: Egypt into last 16 after shootout victory against Australia
items.summary: Egypt defeated Australia on penalties to claim their first ever win in the World Cup knockout stage and set up a last-16 tie with either Argentina or Cape Verde.

items.title: Watch the full shootout as Egypt hold nerve to make World Cup last 16
items.summary: Egypt defeat Australia on penalties to claim their first ever win in the World Cup knockout stage, and make it through to the last 16.

items.title: 'History for the Pharaohs' - Egypt celebrate after winning shootout
items.summary: Egypt celebrate as they win a World Cup knockout match for the first time after beating Australia 4-2 on penalties in Dallas to reach the last 16.

items.summary: EGYPT players were bizarrely gathered around a laptop to watch Real Madrid highlights before their penalty shootout against Australia. items.title: England preparations for Mexico thrown into chaos over kick-off switch proposal with FA livid at World Cup change
items.summary: ENGLAND face their preparations for their last-16 clash with Mexico being thrown into chaos if the kick-off time is moved forward by six hours. items.summary: AUSTRALIA'S extra-time gamble of subbing on a penalty specialist failed to pay off in their World Cup exit to Egypt. items.title: Fifa in talks to move SECOND match after looking to bring forward England kick-off with World Cup in chaos
items.summary: FIFA is scrambling to reshuffle TWO major knockout games, with the last-16 tie between Brazil and Norway at risk of moving. items.title: England fans ‘being treated like dirt’ after ‘outrageous’ decision to move Mexico World Cup clash leaving some stranded
items.summary: ENGLAND fans have hit out at the decision to move the last-16 clash against Mexico. The Three Lions were expected to face Mexico at the Estadio Azteca on Monday at 1am UK time. items.title: FIFA: Croatia VAR call vs. Portugal was correct
items.summary: FIFA has confirmed that Croatia's equaliser against Portugal was correctly ruled out for offside due to technology housed inside the World Cup ball.

items.title: Nagelsmann out as Germany boss, Klopp lined up
items.summary: Julian Nagelsmann is out as the Germany head coach following the team's shock exit from the World Cup in the round of 32, with talks already being sought with Jürgen Klopp to replace him.

items.title: Agent: Chicago Fire willing to let Mbokazi move
items.summary: Chicago Fire FC centre-back Mbekezeli Mbokazi is understood to be attracting interest from Premier League club Nottingham Forest, but ESPN sources have said that no serious offers have been tabled.

items.title: Egypt beat Roos on PKs for 1st-ever knockout win
items.summary: Egypt beat Australia 4-2 in a penalty shootout Friday to secure the nation's first win in a knockout round match at the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Sources: Mexico vs. England to KO six hours early
items.summary: The World Cup match between Mexico and England is likely to have its kickoff time changed due to the risk of weather disruption including flooding, sources told ESPN.
